title: SAC Airstrikes Kill Six During Thingyan New Year — Pauk Township (Magway) and Sagaing Hit
detail: On April 17 — Myanmar New Year's Day (the second day of the Thingyan Water Festival) — SAC military aircraft carried out airstrikes on Pauk Township in Magway Region and Luthaw Township, killing at least six people. The strikes fell during the height of Thingyan celebrations, the four-day Buddhist New Year festival during which millions of Myanmar's Buddhist population traditionally gather in streets and pagodas. Pauk Township in central Magway has been a contested zone since resistance forces expanded PDF operations from Sagaing southward; junta airstrikes in the area have intensified as PDF forces surround SAC-controlled towns and oil infrastructure. The Thingyan period (April 13–16, 2026) saw some of the highest concentrations of civilian casualties from air attacks in recent months, with the SAC documented striking a monastery (April 13, Sagaing), conducting gyrocopter attacks on villages (April 13, Sagaing), and continuing to bomb populated areas even as displaced populations sought shelter. SAC military operations continued without pause throughout the festival period despite international appeals.

media.caption: Airstrikes kill six during Thingyan New Year in Pauk Township as SAC continues operations throughout holiday

title: Junta Thingyan Amnesty Releases RFA Contributor Shin Daewe After 915 Days — 1,350+ Prisoners Freed
detail: As part of a traditional Myanmar New Year prisoner amnesty announced by President Min Aung Hlaing, award-winning documentary filmmaker and Radio Free Asia contributor Shin Daewe was released on April 17, 2026 after 915 days in detention. Shin Daewe had been arrested in October 2023 and sentenced to 15 years in prison on the pretext of purchasing a video drone — widely condemned by press freedom organizations as a politically motivated prosecution. His release was welcomed by colleagues at Insein Prison in Yangon, where many of Myanmar's most prominent political prisoners are held. The broader Thingyan amnesty released over 1,350 prisoners across Myanmar. However, human rights organizations including AAPP noted that the amnesty left tens of thousands of other political prisoners detained; AAPP documents 22,668 political prisoners as of late 2025. Amnesty releases during Thingyan are an annual junta practice often timed to generate positive press coverage while maintaining the vast majority of political detentions. Critics observed that the same government releasing Shin Daewe simultaneously continued airstrikes that killed civilians on April 17 in Pauk Township.

media.caption: RFA contributor Shin Daewe released after 915 days as part of Myanmar New Year prisoner amnesty
